title: BASE file explorer scope, SaaS support admin, folder create
description:
OVH runtime check confirmed Wasabi has 4581 files under portal-users/8 and Mike has portal admin access — the API was fine. The empty BASE explorer was caused by stale URL query params (?clientId= / ?jobId=) overriding the correct shared job (often portal-users/2 or tenant-* from earlier SaaS provisioning work). BASE now always uses resolvePrivateBasePortalScope (portal-users/8 defaults) and strips drifted URL scope on login unless scopeLock=1.

SaaS remote support broke because requireSupportAdmin only allowed Horizon employee admins, not SaaS workspace owners. Tenant purchasers can again see presence for their own tenant only; Mike on BASE still sees all users via listAllPresenceRows.

Folder creation on PipeShare now checks edit permission on the parent folder path (not view on the new child path), which fixes 403s for users with full grants on a parent but no grant row for the not-yet-created subfolder.

Debug instrumentation is included for one verification pass (frontend + backend tree route). Remove after confirmed.

Who is affected: pipeshare.live (BASE), SaaS tenant subdomains (support + folder create). Highlights zoom drift still under separate verification.
changeLog: BASE explorer ignores stale ?clientId/?jobId URL drift; uses portal-users/8 scope.
Strip tenant-* and wrong-job URL params on BASE login (scopeLock=1 to keep deep links).
loadRemoteTree uses live session so tree fetch is not skipped silently.
SaaS workspace owners can administer remote support within their tenant again.
Create-folder API checks parent-path edit grant instead of child-path view grant.
